title.zh: 邊界碎片化下的責任真空：脫歐後北愛爾蘭的治理困境
title.en: The Accountability Void at the Edge of Borders: How Brexit's Fragmented Policies Created a Governance Gap in Northern Ireland

summary.zh:
當代邊界正因外部化與日常邊境檢查而日益碎片化，這種「邊界迷宮」結構讓『誰該為政策影響負責』的問題變得尖銳。作者以脫歐後的歐盟居留計劃為例，聚焦北愛爾蘭——這片英國唯一與歐盟有陸地邊界的特殊區域，揭示了分權、委派與數位化三重機制如何合力製造出難以追責的治理空洞。當北愛爾蘭的邊界政策與英國其他地區脫鉤，私人企業接手申請審核，數位化系統隱形化行政流程，公眾面對權利受損時，卻發現責任主體如霧中花，政府用制度設計巧妙地將責任推給他人，自己卻仍能維持對移民的精準管控。

這套精巧的責任迷宮並非削弱政府控制力，而是刻意設計的「甩鍋機制」：邊界碎片化讓公眾無法對政府提出質問，因為權力分散到不同層級、機構甚至企業，當問題爆發時，每個環節都可推諉「不歸我管」。北愛爾蘭的案例不僅揭示邊界政策的治理危機，更提醒我們：當權力結構為「避責」而設計，民主責任制的根基便開始鬆動。
summary.en:
Contemporary borders have become increasingly fragmented through externalization practices and daily border enforcement, creating a labyrinthine system where accountability for border policies is obscured. Drawing on the EU Settlement Scheme—the policy governing residency rights after Brexit—the study focuses on Northern Ireland, the only part of the UK sharing a land border with the EU, to examine how fragmentation creates accountability gaps through devolution, delegation, and digitization. In Northern Ireland, devolution enabled distinct border procedures that diffused responsibility, while delegation to private entities made it difficult to identify who was accountable for errors in applications. Digitization further complicated the process by rendering administrative decisions opaque, leaving individuals unable to trace or challenge decisions affecting their rights.

This tripartite framework does not reduce the state’s capacity to control migration but instead enables governments to distance themselves from accountability. By fragmenting border regimes across different jurisdictions, actors, and digital systems, authorities can claim ignorance or shift blame, even as they maintain tight control over migration flows. The result is a governance gap where those affected by border policies—like migrants denied residency rights—have no clear avenue to hold power to account. Northern Ireland’s experience underscores a broader crisis: when border policies prioritize control over responsibility, democratic accountability erodes, leaving vulnerable populations without recourse.

keyArticle.title: Devolution, delegation, and digitisation: Accountability gaps in fragmented border regimes
